name: Boy Scout Rule
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 1 & Chapter 17
problem: Code gradually degrades over time because developers only add features and never clean up. Small messes accumulate into large, unmaintainable systems even when everyone follows existing conventions for new code.
steps: Every time you touch a file, leave it slightly cleaner than you found it.
Rename an unclear variable or method if you notice one while making your change.
Extract a long method or remove a redundant comment while you are already in the file.
Keep the improvement small and safe — do not refactor the whole file in one commit.
Add or improve one test if a test is missing for the code you touched.
